title: The Alchemist
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|date=June 14th 2026|Feels more like a DND tumblr OC than an actual fitting individual you'd find in the backrooms. Literally has no use or purpose other than some weird magic despite being a POI and not an entity. Also, former pantheon character. Better fit as a rewrite due to the concept still being usable, we can seperate this from Pantheon withouth deleting it. - PortulTrademark}}
{{Simple Image Block|image=alchemist-new.jpg|caption=A sketch of the PoI by [[Jacob]].}}
== Description == '''The Alchemist''' is an entity PoI with strong alchemicalVia [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alchemy Wikipedia]: ''Alchemists attempted to purify, mature, and perfect certain materials. Common aims were chrysopoeia, the transmutation of "base metals" (e.g., lead) into "noble metals" (particularly gold); the creation of an elixir of immortality; and the creation of panaceas able to cure any disease.'' abilities that bears a resemblance to a human, albeit set apart by distinct and eccentric attributes. His face is somewhat similar to that of a Partial [[Entity 9|Faceling]], in that unsettlingly smooth skin replaces his eyes beneath his glasses. His ears are distinctly pointed, reminiscent of an elf's rather than a human's. His ghastly white complexion and salt-and-pepper beard contrasts with his toothy grin, beaming along with the slight golden sheen that his skin exhibits in direct light. His attire resembles that of a scientist's, a peculiar white lab coat. It's made of loose fabric that gathers at the cuffs and is fastened with golden buttons. It is cinched at the waist with a practical brown belt and a gilded buckle. The Alchemist's coat pocket most commonly includes two items: a pen, and a magnifying glass. These items signify the Alchemist's curiosity and propensity for science, though only the pen is used by him frequentlyWhen questioned on his lack of use of the magnifying glass, he said he "doesn't need it"..
== Behaviours == The Alchemist is an eccentric personality, and can be very unpredictable. He carries himself in an enthusiastic manner even at the worst of times, caring very little for the circumstances around him, being remarkably unaffected by shocking or tragic events. His manner of speaking is equal parts scientific and enthusiastic, and can often come across as quite condescending. His attitude to alchemy is one of great pride, coupled with a genuine passion for the craft. He had this to say on his more eccentric personality in comparison to other godlike entities: ''"Did I used to be... Godlike? You mean formal, not Godlike. Either way, sure I did! But that was when humans didn't have science like you have today. I got to work things out for you, and you all thought I was great. And that's how it went for years and years until you all got too good at my game. So what's a man to do, pretend to be your babysitters when you're all perfectly fine on your own? Preposterous. That's the Keymaster's job."'' {{Simple Image Block|image=Gold Alchemist.jpg|caption=A gold nugget created by the Alchemist from a chunk of lead.}} The Alchemist's main ability is, according to him, the ability to "understand anything, down to its most basic systems" — this apparently can mean anything from seeing things' insides without opening them to comprehending and editing their molecular structures. His expertise can be enlisted in return for a favour; procurement of items, the teaching of interesting information, or even the revival of the dead could be possible if he chooses to entertain your request. The favours and trades he asks for in return vary from trivial to substantial, for example: * Human blood (withdrawn from a syringe he keeps inside his coat); * Busywork, for example, "Get me a doughnut;" * Whisky; * Your mortal soul; * Human currency which he apparently collects; * A quarter of your personality attempts by the Alchemist (or the wanderer after giving the said 'quarter' up) to explain what is meant by a half of a personality are unhelpful and incoherent.; * A cigarette. If his request is too difficult to fulfil, you might be better served to ask for his help on another day when he's in a better mood.
== Biology == The Alchemist's biology is a mystery through and through. He is able to see despite a lack of eyes, even wearing glasses over the space where his eyes would be. His vision is reportedly precise and far-reaching, unlike his hearing which (despite his ears being an irregularly pointed shape) is equivalent to a human's in range. Through a manual, painstaking process the Alchemist can rapidly heal substantial injuries. This makes him practically invulnerable, though he still exhibits an aversion to risk due to the pain and effort of healing.
== Discovery == The Alchemist's origins are currently unknown. The Alchemist is, nowadays, not an entirely uncommon sighting, though he may be elusive if you aim to find him for yourself. He tends to stay in safer levels, such as [[Level 10]].
== Do's and Don'ts ==
=== Do ===
* '''Do''' ask the Alchemist to procure items for you, if the favour he asks in return is reasonable. * '''Do''' keep clear of the Alchemist if you don't need anything from him. Interactions are unpleasurable and ill-advised.
=== Don't ===
* '''Don't''' waste time arguing with the Alchemist, or attempt to build a rapport. * '''Don't''' accept a bargain you can't hold up. It will cost you dearly.
